# dotfiles

> Cross-platform dotfiles managed by Chezmoi + Nix for macOS and Linux. Provides one-command environment bootstrap with Nix development shells (Node, Python, Rust, Go, DevOps, Haskell, Ruby, and more), Zsh + Oh My Zsh + Powerlevel10k, Tmux with Catppuccin theme, Neovim with full LSP support, Homebrew extras for macOS, and a suite of portable AI agent skills in the `skills/` directory.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/README.md): Setup, dev environments, shell/terminal/editor config, and automation
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/AGENTS.md): File naming conventions, key directories, and commit guidelines

## API / Key Files

- [flake.nix](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/flake.nix): Nix flake with dev shells (default, node, python, rust, go, devops, haskell, ruby, scheme, perl, lua, full)
- [justfile](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/justfile): Task runner (update, status, check)
- [Brewfile](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/Brewfile): macOS Homebrew packages
- [dot_config/](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/dot_config/): Neovim, Ghostty, Tmux, and other tool configs
- [dot_zshrc.tmpl](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/dot_zshrc.tmpl): Zsh configuration template
- [skills/](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/): Portable AI agent skills directory

## Skills

- [ai-workflows](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/ai-workflows/SKILL.md): AI-assisted workflow patterns
- [ci-cd](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/ci-cd/SKILL.md): CI/CD pipeline conventions
- [cli-patterns](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/cli-patterns/SKILL.md): CLI design patterns
- [cli-ui](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/cli-ui/SKILL.md): Terminal UI standard (colors, spinners, symbols, layout)
- [create-oss-skill](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/create-oss-skill/SKILL.md): Agent skill scaffolding and authoring
- [development-practices](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/development-practices/SKILL.md): Coding standards and practices
- [llms-txt](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/llms-txt/SKILL.md): LLMs.txt generation guidance
- [memory](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/memory/SKILL.md): Persistent learning from user patterns and preferences
- [nix-shells](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/nix-shells/SKILL.md): Nix development shell guidance
- [pragmatic-programming](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/pragmatic-programming/SKILL.md): Pragmatic programming principles
- [project-scaffolding](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/project-scaffolding/SKILL.md): Project scaffolding conventions
- [readme-standards](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/readme-standards/SKILL.md): README standards
- [release-workflow](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/release-workflow/SKILL.md): Release workflow conventions
- [tools](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/tools/SKILL.md): Canonical tech stack reference
- [visual-identity](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/skills/visual-identity/SKILL.md): Branding and visual standards

## Optional

- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/urmzd/dotfiles/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute
